query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: c0066b4a).

## Issue to Resolve

Remove default for allowed wildcard imports in `ktlint_official` codestyle
>Expected Behavior
> When java.util.* is included as a wildcard import, it should trigger the warning for no-wildcard-imports.
> ...
> Given that, I think it makes sense that ktlint should stop including those two paths as default wildcard exceptions.

_Originally posted by @greg-patterson in https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1792#issuecomment-1407098815_

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

The feature to respect setting ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand  was implemented not too long ago in ktlint 0.45.x via https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1272. The reason for adding the default values is that they seems to be hard-coded in IntelliJ IDEA as well (https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82).

If the property is not set explicitly, IntelliJ IDEA allows imports java.util.* and kotlinx.android.synthetic.*. If ktlint would not do the same, this would result in a conclict between ktlint and IntelliJ IDEA. Lots of users of ktlint dislike that.

In the new codestyle `ktlint_official` the default value for allowed wildcard imports shoulbe be cleared.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `.editorconfig`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `docs/rules/standard.md`
- `ktlint-cli/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/cli/internal/KtlintCommandLine.kt`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/RuleExecutionContext.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this issue. The problem is about wildcard imports in ktlint's `ktlint_official` code style. Currently, there are default allowed wildcard imports (`java.util.*` and `kotlinx.android.synthetic.*`) that are hardcoded based on IntelliJ IDEA's defaults. However, for the `ktlint_official` code style, these defaults should be cleared/removed.

Let me start by exploring the relevant files to understand the current implementation.

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.importordering.PatternEntry
  9→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
 12→
 13→public class NoWildcardImportsRule :
 14→    StandardRule(
 15→        id = "no-wildcard-imports",
 16→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 17→            IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND,
 18→        ),
 19→    ) {
 20→    private lateinit var allowedWildcardImports: List<PatternEntry>
 21→
 22→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 23→        allowedWildcardImports = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND]
 24→    }
 25→
 26→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 27→        node: ASTNode,
 28→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 29→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 30→    ) {
 31→        if (node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
 32→            val importDirective = node.psi as KtImportDirective
 33→            val path = importDirective.importPath ?: return
 34→            if (!path.isAllUnder) return
 35→            if (allowedWildcardImports.none { it.matches(path) }) {
 36→                emit(node.startOffset, "Wildcard import", false)
 37→            }
 38→        }
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    public companion object {
 42→        private const val WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES = "*"
 43→        private const val WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES = "**"
 44→
 45→        private fun parseAllowedWildcardImports(allowedWildcardImports: String): List<PatternEntry> {
 46→            val importsList = allowedWildcardImports.split(",").onEach { it.trim() }
 47→
 48→            return importsList.map { import ->
 49→                if (import.endsWith(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES)) { // java.**
 50→                    PatternEntry(
 51→                        packageName = import.removeSuffix(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES).plus(WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES),
 52→                        withSubpackages = true,
 53→                        hasAlias = false,
 54→                    )
 55→                } else {
 56→                    PatternEntry(
 57→                        packageName = import,
 58→                        withSubpackages = false,
 59→                        hasAlias = false,
 60→                    )
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        private val PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
 66→            { _, value ->
 67→                when {
 68→                    else -> try {
 69→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
 70→                            value,
 71→                            value?.let(Companion::parseAllowedWildcardImports) ?: emptyList(),
 72→                        )
 73→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 74→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
 75→                            value,
 76→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
 77→                        )
 78→                    }
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→
 82→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
 83→            EditorConfigProperty(
 84→                type = PropertyType(
 85→                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
 86→                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
 87→                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
 88→                ),
 89→                /**
 90→                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
 91→                 * it's subpackages.
 92→                 *
 93→                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
 94→                 */
 95→                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
 96→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
 97→            )
 98→    }
 99→}
100→
101→public val NO_WILDCARD_IMPORTS_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoWildcardImportsRule().ruleId
102→

16→ */
17→public data class EditorConfigProperty<T>(
18→    /**

20→     */
21→    public val type: PropertyType<T>,

23→    /**

25→     * unless the code style specific default value is set.
26→     */
27→    public val defaultValue: T,
28→
29→    /**
30→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'ktlint_official'. When not
31→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
32→     */
33→    public val ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
34→
35→    /**
36→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'intellij_idea'. When not
37→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
38→     */
39→    public val intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
40→
41→    /**
42→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'android_studio'. When not
43→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
44→     */
45→    public val androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
46→
47→    /**
48→     * If set, it maps the actual value set for the property, to another valid value for that property. See example
49→     * below where
50→     * ```kotlin
51→     * propertyMapper = { property, isAndroidCodeStyle ->
52→     *     when {
53→     *         property == null ->
54→     *             // property is not defined in ".editorconfig" file
55→     *         property.isUnset ->
56→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with special value "unset"
57→     *         property.sourceValue == "some-string-value" ->
58→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with a value that needs to be remapped to another
59→     *             // valid value. For example the "max_line_length" property accepts value "off" but is remapped to
60→     *             // "-1" in ktlint.
61→     *        else ->
62→     *             property.getValueAs() // or null
63→     *     }
64→     * }
65→     * ```
66→     * In case the lambda returns a null value then, the code style specific default value or the generic [defaultValue]
67→     * is used.
68→     */
69→    public val propertyMapper: ((Property?, CodeStyleValue) -> T?)? = null,
70→
71→    /**

content.thinking:
Now I understand the architecture. The `EditorConfigProperty` class has a parameter `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` that allows specifying a different default value for the `ktlint_official` code style.

Looking at the `NoWildcardImportsRule`, the `IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND` property has:
- `defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**")`

But it doesn't specify a `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue`, so it defaults to the same value.

According to the issue, for the `ktlint_official` code style, the default value should be cleared (empty list).

Let me modify the `NoWildcardImportsRule.kt` to add `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList()` to the `IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND` property.

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. The `EditorConfigProperty` class allows specifying code style-specific default values through the `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` parameter. The `NoWildcardImportsRule` currently has default wildcard imports (`java.util.*` and `kotlinx.android.synthetic.**`) but doesn't specify a different default for the `ktlint_official` code style.

According to the issue, for the `ktlint_official` code style, the default value should be an empty list (no allowed wildcard imports by default).

Let me also check how the EditorConfig retrieves the property values to ensure I understand the mechanism:

  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  5→import mu.KotlinLogging
  6→import org.ec4j.core.model.Property
  7→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
  8→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValue
  9→
 10→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * Loaded [Property]s from `.editorconfig` files.
 14→ */
 15→public data class EditorConfig(
 16→    private val properties: Map<String, Property> = emptyMap(),
 17→) {
 18→    public constructor(vararg properties: Property) : this(
 19→        properties.associateBy { property -> property.name },
 20→    )
 21→
 22→    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue
 23→        get() =
 24→            with(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY) {
 25→                // Prevent printing a lot of warnings when the code_style_value is not defined explicitly by not using the function to get
 26→                // the default value for the property
 27→                type
 28→                    .getPropertyValue(type.name)
 29→                    .parsed
 30→                    ?: defaultValue
 31→            }
 32→
 33→    /**
 34→     * Gets the value of [editorConfigProperty] from [EditorConfig] provided that the name of the property is identical to the name of the
 35→     * type of the property. If the value is not found, the default value for the matching code style is returned.
 36→     */
 37→    public operator fun <T> get(editorConfigProperty: EditorConfigProperty<T>): T {
 38→        when {
 39→            editorConfigProperty.deprecationError != null ->
 40→                throw DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(
 41→                    "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is disallowed: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationError}",
 42→                )
 43→
 44→            editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning != null ->
 45→                LOGGER.warn { "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is deprecated: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning}" }
 46→        }
 47→        val property = properties.getOrElse(editorConfigProperty.name) {
 48→            throw IllegalStateException(
 49→                "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' can not be retrieved from this EditorConfig. Note that the EditorConfig which " +
 50→                    "is provided to class '${Rule::class.qualifiedName}' only contains the properties which are defined in the property " +
 51→                    "'${Rule::usesEditorConfigProperties.name}'.",
 52→            )
 53→        }
 54→
 55→        editorConfigProperty
 56→            .propertyMapper
 57→            ?.invoke(property, codeStyle)
 58→            ?.let { newValue ->
 59→                // If the property value is remapped to a non-null value then return it immediately.
 60→                val originalValue = property.sourceValue
 61→                if (newValue.toString() != originalValue) {
 62→                    LOGGER.trace {
 63→                        "Value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is remapped " +
 64→                            "from '$originalValue' to '$newValue'"
 65→                    }
 66→                }
 67→                return newValue
 68→            }
 69→
 70→        return if (property.isUnset) {
 71→            editorConfigProperty
 72→                .getDefaultValue()
 73→                .also {
 74→                    LOGGER.trace {
 75→                        "No value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' was found. Value has been defaulted to " +
 76→                            "'$it'. Setting the value explicitly in '.editorconfig' removes this message from the log."
 77→                    }
 78→                }
 79→        } else {
 80→            // In case the EditorConfig is loaded incorrectly it can happen that the type field of the loaded property is null. Getting the
 81→            // value via "property.getValueAs()" can lead to a class cast exception as the actual type of the value returned is transformed
 82→            // to a String instead of type T. Getting the value via the editorConfigProperty type is safe as it can not be null.
 83→            editorConfigProperty.getParsedValueOrDefault() ?: editorConfigProperty.getDefaultValue()
 84→        }
 85→    }
 86→
 87→    /**
 88→     * Gets the value of the property with [propertyType] and name [propertyName] from [EditorConfig]. Returns null if the type is not
 89→     * found. Also, returns null when no property with the name is found.
 90→     */
 91→    public fun <T> getEditorConfigValueOrNull(
 92→        propertyType: PropertyType<T>,
 93→        propertyName: String,
 94→    ): T? = propertyType.getPropertyValue(propertyName).parsed
 95→
 96→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getDefaultValue() =
 97→        when (codeStyle) {
 98→            CodeStyleValue.android -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
 99→            CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
100→            CodeStyleValue.official -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
101→            CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
102→            CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue
103→        }
104→
105→    /**
106→     * Checks whether a property with name [propertyName] is defined.
107→     */
108→    public fun contains(propertyName: String): Boolean = properties.contains(propertyName)
109→
110→    /**
111→     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType]. Returns null if the property type is not found. Can only be used for
112→     * properties for which the name of the property is identical to the name of the type.
113→     */
114→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(): T? =
115→        this.type.getParsedValueOrDefault(this.type.name, this.defaultValue)
116→
117→    /**
118→     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType] and name [propertyName]. Returns null if the property type is not
119→     * found. Intended to be used for properties for which the name is not identical to the name of the property type.
120→     */
121→    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(
122→        propertyName: String,
123→        defaultValue: T,
124→    ): T =
125→        getPropertyValue(propertyName)
126→            .let { propertyValue ->
127→                if (propertyValue.isValid) {
128→                    propertyValue.parsed
129→                } else {
130→                    LOGGER.warn {
131→                        "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' contains an invalid value '${propertyValue.source}'. The default value " +
132→                            "'$defaultValue' is used instead."
133→                    }
134→                    defaultValue
135→                }
136→            }
137→
138→    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getPropertyValue(propertyName: String): PropertyValue<T> =
139→        parse(
140→            properties[propertyName]?.sourceValue,
141→        )
142→
143→    /**
144→     * Maps all properties with given [mapper] to a collection.
145→     */
146→    public fun <T> map(mapper: (Property) -> T): Collection<T> =
147→        properties
148→            .values
149→            .map { mapper(it) }
150→
151→    /**
152→     * Adds the given [EditorConfigProperty]'s with its default value for the active code style to the [EditorConfig] if the property does
153→     * not yet exist.
154→     */
155→    public fun addPropertiesWithDefaultValueIfMissing(vararg defaultEditorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperty<*>): EditorConfig {
156→        val editorConfigProperties = defaultEditorConfigProperties.toSet()
157→        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
158→        return editorConfigProperties
159→            .defaultProperties()
160→            .plus(
161→                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
162→                properties,
163→            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
164→    }
165→
166→    /**
167→     * Creates an [EditorConfig] containing [editorConfigProperties] only. Retains the value of the property when defined in the original
168→     * [EditorConfig]. If the property does not exist in the original [EditorConfig], it will be added and its value is set to the default
169→     * value of the active code style.
170→     */
171→    public fun filterBy(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>): EditorConfig {
172→        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
173→        return editorConfigProperties
174→            .defaultProperties()
175→            .plus(
176→                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
177→                properties.filterBy(editorConfigProperties.map { it.name }),
178→            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
179→    }
180→
181→    private fun requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>) {
182→        val editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities =
183→            editorConfigProperties
184→                .groupBy { it.name }
185→                .filterValues { groupedEditorConfigProperties ->
186→                    groupedEditorConfigProperties
187→                        .distinctBy { it.hashCode() }
188→                        .count() > 1
189→                }
190→        require(editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities.isEmpty()) {
191→            editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities
192→                .map { (name, editorConfigProperties) ->
193→                    editorConfigProperties
194→                        .joinToString(
195→                            separator = "\n",
196→                            prefix = "Found multiple editorconfig properties with name '$name' but having distinct identities:\n",
197→                        ) { "  - $it" }
198→                }.joinToString(separator = "\n")
199→        }
200→    }
201→
202→    private fun Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>.defaultProperties(): Map<String, Property> {
203→        return associate { editorConfigProperty ->
204→            editorConfigProperty
205→                .writeDefaultValue()
206→                .let { editorConfigProperty.name to editorConfigProperty.toPropertyWithValue(it) }
207→        }
208→    }
209→
210→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.writeDefaultValue() = propertyWriter(getDefaultValue())
211→
212→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.toPropertyWithValue(value: String): Property =
213→        Property
214→            .builder()
215→            .type(type)
216→            .name(name)
217→            .value(value)
218→            .build()
219→
220→    private fun Map<String, Property>.filterBy(editorConfigProperties: List<String>): Map<String, Property> =
221→        filterKeys { it in editorConfigProperties }
222→}
223→
224→private class DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(message: String) : RuntimeException(message)
225→

Now I can see the full picture. The key line is in `getDefaultValue()`:

```kotlin
private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getDefaultValue() =
    when (codeStyle) {
        CodeStyleValue.android -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
        CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
        CodeStyleValue.official -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
        CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
        CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue
    }
```

So when the code style is `ktlint_official`, it uses `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Currently in `NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`, the `IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND` property doesn't specify `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue`, so it defaults to the same value as `defaultValue`.

The fix is simple: I need to add `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList()` to the `IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND` property definition.

Let me make this change.

  9→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
 12→
 13→public class NoWildcardImportsRule :
 14→    StandardRule(
 15→        id = "no-wildcard-imports",
 16→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 17→            IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND,
 18→        ),
 19→    ) {
 20→    private lateinit var allowedWildcardImports: List<PatternEntry>
 21→
 22→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 23→        allowedWildcardImports = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND]
 24→    }
 25→
 26→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 27→        node: ASTNode,
 28→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 29→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 30→    ) {
 31→        if (node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
 32→            val importDirective = node.psi as KtImportDirective
 33→            val path = importDirective.importPath ?: return
 34→            if (!path.isAllUnder) return
 35→            if (allowedWildcardImports.none { it.matches(path) }) {
 36→                emit(node.startOffset, "Wildcard import", false)
 37→            }
 38→        }
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    public companion object {
 42→        private const val WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES = "*"
 43→        private const val WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES = "**"
 44→
 45→        private fun parseAllowedWildcardImports(allowedWildcardImports: String): List<PatternEntry> {
 46→            val importsList = allowedWildcardImports.split(",").onEach { it.trim() }
 47→
 48→            return importsList.map { import ->
 49→                if (import.endsWith(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES)) { // java.**
 50→                    PatternEntry(
 51→                        packageName = import.removeSuffix(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES).plus(WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES),
 52→                        withSubpackages = true,
 53→                        hasAlias = false,
 54→                    )
 55→                } else {
 56→                    PatternEntry(
 57→                        packageName = import,
 58→                        withSubpackages = false,
 59→                        hasAlias = false,
 60→                    )
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        private val PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
 66→            { _, value ->
 67→                when {
 68→                    else -> try {
 69→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
 70→                            value,
 71→                            value?.let(Companion::parseAllowedWildcardImports) ?: emptyList(),
 72→                        )
 73→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 74→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
 75→                            value,
 76→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
 77→                        )
 78→                    }
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→
 82→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
 83→            EditorConfigProperty(
 84→                type = PropertyType(
 85→                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
 86→                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
 87→                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
 88→                ),
 89→                /**
 90→                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
 91→                 * it's subpackages.
 92→                 *
 93→                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
 94→                 */
 95→                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
 96→                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList(),
 97→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
 98→            )
 99→    }
100→}

135→        code

281→    public fun hasLintViolationsWithoutAutoCorrect(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): Unit =
282→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(*expectedErrors)
283→
284→    private fun ktLintAssertThatAssertable(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
285→        if (editorConfigProperties.isEmpty()) {
286→            KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
287→                ruleProvider = ruleProvider,
288→                code = code(),
289→                editorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
290→                additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toSet(),
291→            )
292→        } else {
293→            KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
294→                ruleProvider = ruleProvider,
295→                code = code(),
296→                editorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.from(*editorConfigProperties.toTypedArray()),
297→                additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toSet(),
298→            )
299→        }
300→
301→    private fun code() =
302→        if (filePath != null) {
303→            // Create a file containing the code and the given filepath. CodeFile can not be used here as it can not read successfully from
304→            // the Ktlint test file system.
305→            val path = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.resolve(filePath)
306→            KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.writeFile(path.pathString, code)
307→            Code.fromPath(path)
308→        } else {
309→            Code.fromSnippet(code, kotlinScript)
310→        }
311→
312→    public companion object {
313→        /**
314→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
315→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules.
316→         */
317→        public fun assertThatRule(provider: () -> Rule): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat()
318→
319→        /**
320→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
321→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules. The rules provided via [additionalRuleProviders] are only
322→         * executed during the format phase of the test. This means that the unit test only has to check the lint
323→         * violations thrown by the rule for which the assertThat is created. But the code is formatted by both the rule
324→         * and the rules provided by [additionalRuleProviders] in the order as defined by the rule definitions.
325→         */
326→
327→        public fun assertThatRule(
328→            provider: () -> Rule,
329→            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
330→        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat(additionalRuleProviders)
331→
332→        private fun RuleProvider.assertThat(additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet()): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat =
333→            { code ->
334→                KtLintAssertThat(
335→                    ruleProvider = this,
336→                    code = code,
337→                    additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toMutableSet(),
338→                )
339→            }
340→
341→        /**
342→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
343→         */
344→        public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER: String = "Max line length marker:"
345→
346→        /**
347→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
348→         */
349→        public const val EOL_CHAR: Char = '#'
350→
351→        init {
352→            /* A unit test using the max line length marker typically looks like:
353→             * val code =
354→             *     """
355→             *     // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
356→             *     val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
357→             *     """.trimIndent()
358→             * In order for this to work properly, the length of "$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER" must be identical to the length of the real value
359→             * of the constant MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.
360→             */
361→            require("$".plus(::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name).length == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.length) {
362→                "Length of the value of the string '${'$'}MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' must be identical to length of the value of the " +
363→                    "constant. Tests using this constant will not work as intended otherwise."
364→            }
365→        }
366→    }
367→}
368→
369→/**
370→ * Immutable assertable. Once the first assertion is made on [KtLintAssertThat] it is converted to the
371→ * [KtLintAssertThatAssertable] which allows no further modifications of the internal state. This guarantees that all
372→ * assertions operate on the same state.
373→ */
374→public class KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
375→    /** The provider of the rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created. */
376→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
377→    private val code: Code,
378→    private val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
379→    /**
380→     *  The rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note that
381→     *  lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
382→     */
383→    private val additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
384→) : AbstractAssert<KtLintAssertThatAssertable, String>(code.content, KtLintAssertThatAssertable::class.java) {
385→    private val ruleId = ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance().ruleId
386→
387→    /**
388→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s caused by the rule associated with the
389→     * KtLintAssertThat.
390→     *
391→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
392→     */
393→    public fun hasNoLintViolations() {
394→        assertThat(lint().filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
395→
396→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
397→        val (actualFormattedCode, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
398→
399→        assertAll(
400→            {
401→                assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting)
402→                    .describedAs(
403→                        "LintViolations found by format while no lint errors were found during Lint. " +
404→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
405→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
406→                            } else {
407→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
408→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
409→                            },
410→                    )
411→                    .isEmpty()
412→            },
413→            {
414→                assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
415→                    .describedAs(
416→                        "Code is changed by format while no lint errors were found. " +
417→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
418→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
419→                            } else {
420→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
421→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
422→                            },
423→                    )
424→                    .isEqualTo(code.content)
425→            },
426→        )
427→    }
428→
429→    /**
430→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for the given rule id.
431→     */
432→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
433→        val (_, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
434→
435→        assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting.filter { it.ruleId == ruleId })
436→            .describedAs("At least 1 lint violation was found for rule id '$ruleId' while none were expected")
437→            .isEmpty()
438→
439→        return this
440→    }
441→
442→    /**
443→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s except in the additional formatting rules.
444→     *
445→     * Note that this method can and should be chained with [isFormattedAs] to verify whether the code is correctly
446→     * formatted.
447→     *
448→     * This method can be used when the rule which is associated with the KtLintAssertThat is not violated by the sample
449→     * code, but the code is reformatted by the additional formatting rules. In case that rules are dependent on each
450→     * other, a unit test cal still verify that code is formatted correctly even when the rule under test is not
451→     * violated.
452→     */
453→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
454→        check(additionalRuleProviders.isNotEmpty()) {
455→            "hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules can only be used when additional rules have been added"
456→        }
457→
458→        val lintErrors = lint()
459→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
460→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterAdditionalRulesOnly()).isNotEmpty
461→
462→        return this
463→    }
464→
465→    /**
466→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]. This is a sugar coated version of
467→     * [hasLintViolation] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
468→     */
469→    public fun hasLintViolation(
470→        line: Int,
471→        col: Int,
472→        detail: String,
473→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
474→        hasLintViolations(
475→            LintViolation(
476→                line = line,
477→                col = col,
478→                detail = detail,
479→            ),
480→        )
481→
482→    /**
483→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] caused by an additional rule. This is a sugar coated
484→     * version of [hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
485→     */
486→    public fun hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(
487→        line: Int,
488→        col: Int,
489→        detail: String,
490→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
491→        hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(
492→            LintViolation(
493→                line = line,
494→                col = col,
495→                detail = detail,
496→            ),
497→        )
498→
499→    /**
500→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s which can be automatically corrected.
501→     */
502→    public fun hasLintViolations(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
503→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
504→
505→        val actualLintViolationFields =
506→            lint()
507→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
508→                .toLintViolationsFields()
509→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
510→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
511→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields(canBeAutoCorrected = true))
512→        return this
513→    }
514→
515→    /**
516→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s caused by additional rules and which can be
517→     * automatically corrected.
518→     */
519→    public fun hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
520→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
521→
522→        val actualLintViolationFields =
523→            lint()
524→                .filterAdditionalRulesOnly()
525→                .toLintViolationsFields()
526→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
527→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
528→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields(canBeAutoCorrected = true))
529→        return this
530→    }
531→
532→    /**
533→     * Asserts that the code is formatted as given.
534→     */
535→    public fun isFormattedAs(formattedCode: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
536→        check(formattedCode != code.content) {
537→            "Use '.hasNoLintViolations()' instead of '.isFormattedAs(<original code>)'"
538→        }
539→
540→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
541→        val (actualFormattedCode, _) = format()
542→
543→        assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
544→            .describedAs("Code is formatted as")
545→            .isEqualTo(formattedCode)
546→
547→        return this
548→    }
549→
550→    /**
551→     * Asserts that the code contains the given [LintViolation] which can not be automatically corrected.
552→     */
553→    public fun hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(
554→        line: Int,
555→        col: Int,
556→        detail: String,
557→    ): Unit =
558→        hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(
559→            LintViolation(
560→                line = line,
561→                col = col,
562→                detail = detail,
563→            ),
564→        )
565→
566→    /**
567→     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation]s and that those violations can not be automatically
568→     * corrected.
569→     */
570→    public fun hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(vararg expectedLintViolations: LintViolation) {
571→        check(expectedLintViolations.isNotEmpty())
572→
573→        val actualLintViolationFields =
574→            lint()
575→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
576→                .toLintViolationsFields()
577→
578→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
579→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can not be automatically corrected")
580→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedLintViolations.toLintViolationsFields(canBeAutoCorrected = false))
581→    }
582→
583→    private fun Array<out LintViolation>.toLintViolationsFields(canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean): Array<LintViolationFields> {
584→        return map {
585→            LintViolationFields(
586→                line = it.line,
587→                col = it.col,
588→                detail = it.detail,
589→                canBeAutoCorrected = canBeAutoCorrected,
590→            )
591→        }.toTypedArray()
592→    }
593→
594→    private fun List<LintError>.filterAdditionalRulesOnly() = filter { it.ruleId != ruleId.value }
595→
596→    private fun List<LintError>.filterCurrentRuleOnly() = filter { it.ruleId == ruleId.value }
597→
598→    private fun List<LintError>.toLintViolationsFields(): Array<LintViolationFields> =
599→        map {
600→            LintViolationFields(
601→                line = it.line,
602→                col = it.col,
603→                detail = it.detail,
604→                canBeAutoCorrected = it.canBeAutoCorrected,
605→            )
606→        }.toTypedArray()
607→
608→    private fun createKtLintRuleEngine(): KtLintRuleEngine {
609→        val ruleProviders =
610→            setOf(ruleProvider)
611→                // Also run the additional rules as the main rule might have a VisitorModifier which requires one or more of
612→                // the additional rules to be loaded and enabled as well.
613→                .plus(additionalRuleProviders)
614→                .plus(DUMP_AST_RULE_PROVIDER)
615→        val editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride
616→            .enableExperimentalRules()
617→            .extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders)
618→        return KtLintRuleEngine(
619→            ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
620→            editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride,
621→            fileSystem = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.fileSystem,
622→        )
623→    }
624→
625→    private fun lint(): List<LintError> {
626→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
627→        createKtLintRuleEngine().lint(code) { lintError -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
628→        return lintErrors
629→    }
630→
631→    private fun format(): Pair<String, List<LintError>> {
632→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
633→        val formattedCode = createKtLintRuleEngine().format(code) { lintError, _ -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
634→        return Pair(formattedCode, lintErrors)
635→    }
636→
637→    /* Representation of the field of the [LintError] that should be identical. Note that no comparison can be made
638→     * against the original [LintError] as the [canBeAutoCorrected] flag is excluded from the hashcode.
639→     */
640→    private data class LintViolationFields(
641→        val line: Int,
642→        val col: Int,
643→        val detail: String,
644→        val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean,
645→    )
646→}
647→
648→internal class MissingEolMarker : RuntimeException(
649→    """
650→    The first line of the provide code sample should contain text '$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' which is provided by the
651→    constant '${::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name}' and ends with the EOL_CHAR'$EOL_CHAR' provided by the constant
652→    '${::EOL_CHAR.name}' which indicates the last position that is allowed.
653→    """.trimIndent(),
654→)
655→
656→/**
657→ * Expectation of the [LintError]. Contrary to the [LintError] it does not contain the ruleId. The ruleId will be
658→ * derived from the rule for which the AssertThat was created.
659→ */
660→public data class LintViolation(
661→    val line: Int,
662→    val col: Int,
663→    val detail: String,
664→)
665→
666→/**
667→ * Enables the rule sets for the given set of [ruleProviders] unless the rule execution of that rule set was already
668→ * provided.
669→ */
670→private fun EditorConfigOverride.extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigOverride {
671→    val ruleSetRuleExecutions = ruleProviders
672→        .asSequence()
673→        .map { ruleProvider ->
674→            ruleProvider
675→                .createNewRuleInstance()
676→                .ruleId
677→                .ruleSetId
678→                .createRuleSetExecutionEditorConfigProperty()
679→        }.distinct()
680→        .filter { editorConfigProperty -> this.properties[editorConfigProperty] == null }
681→        .map { it to RuleExecution.enabled }
682→        .toList()
683→        .toTypedArray()
684→    return this.plus(*ruleSetRuleExecutions)
685→}
686→
687→private fun EditorConfigOverride.enableExperimentalRules(): EditorConfigOverride =
688→    plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)
689→

I see the test framework and it looks like by default the test framework doesn't set a specific code style, so the default code style (intellij_idea) is used. To test the ktlint_official code style behavior, we need to use `.withEditorConfigOverride(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY to ktlint_official)`.

The existing tests use the default code style (intellij_idea), which means they use the default wildcard imports (`java.util.*` and `kotlinx.android.synthetic.**`). My change adds `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList()`, which means when using `ktlint_official` code style, no wildcard imports are allowed by default.

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8→
  9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13→
 14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15→
 16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17→
 18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22→
 23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24→
 25→#### Experimental rules
 26→
 27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28→
 29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30→
 31→#### EditorConfig
 32→
 33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34→
 35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36→
 37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38→
 39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40→
 41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42→
 43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44→
 45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46→
 47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48→
 49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50→
 51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 52→
 53→Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine`:
 54→
 55→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
 56→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 57→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
 58→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1→## Annotation formatting
  2→
  3→Multiple annotations should be on a separate line than the annotated declaration; annotations with parameters should each be on separate lines; annotations should be followed by a space
  4→
  5→Rule-id: `annotation`
  6→
  7→## Argument list wrapping
  8→
  9→Rule-id: `argument-list-wrapping`
 10→
 11→## Chain wrapping
 12→
 13→When wrapping chained calls `.`, `?.` and `?:` should be placed on the next line
 14→
 15→Rule id: `chain-wrapping`
 16→
 17→## Enum entry
 18→
 19→Enum entry names should be uppercase underscore-separated names.
 20→
 21→Rule id: `enum-entry-name-case`
 22→
 23→## File name
 24→
 25→Files containing only one toplevel domain should be named according to that element.
 26→
 27→Rule id: `filename`
 28→
 29→## Final newline
 30→
 31→Ensures consistent usage of a newline at the end of each file. 
 32→
 33→This rule can be configured with `.editorconfig` property [`insert_final_newline`](../configuration-ktlint/#final-newline).
 34→
 35→Rule id: `final-newline`
 36→
 37→## Import ordering
 38→
 39→Imports ordered consistently (see [Custom ktlint EditorConfig properties](#custom-ktlint-specific-editorconfig-properties) for more)
 40→
 41→Rule id: `import-ordering`
 42→
 43→## Indentation
 44→
 45→Indentation formatting - respects `.editorconfig` `indent_size` with no continuation indent (see [EditorConfig](../configuration-ktlint/) section for more).
 46→
 47→Rule id: `indent`
 48→
 49→## Max line length
 50→
 51→Ensures that lines do not exceed the given length of `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` (see [EditorConfig](../configuration-ktlint/) section for more). This rule does not apply in a number of situations. For example, in the case a line exceeds the maximum line length due to and comment that disables ktlint rules than that comment is being ignored when validating the length of the line. The `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier` can be set to ignore identifiers which are enclosed in backticks, which for example is very useful when you want to allow longer names for unit tests.
 52→
 53→Rule id: `max-line-length`
 54→
 55→## Modifier order
 56→
 57→Consistent order of modifiers
 58→
 59→Rule id: `modifier-order`
 60→
 61→## Multiline if-else
 62→
 63→Braces required for multiline if/else statements.
 64→
 65→Rule id: `multiline-if-else`
 66→
 67→## No blank lines before `}`
 68→
 69→No blank lines before `}`.
 70→
 71→Rule id: `no-blank-line-before-rbrace`
 72→
 73→## No blank lines in chained method calls
 74→
 75→Rule id: `no-blank-lines-in-chained-method-calls`
 76→
 77→## No consecutive blank lines
 78→ 
 79→Rule id: `no-consecutive-blank-lines`
 80→
 81→## No empty (`{}`) class bodies
 82→
 83→Rule id: `no-empty-class-body`
 84→
 85→## No leading empty lines in method blocks
 86→ 
 87→Rule id: `no-empty-first-line-in-method-block`
 88→
 89→## No line break after else
 90→
 91→Disallows line breaks after the else keyword if that could lead to confusion, for example:
 92→```kotlin
 93→if (conditionA()) {
 94→    doSomething()
 95→} else
 96→if (conditionB()) {
 97→    doAnotherThing()
 98→}
 99→```
100→
101→Rule id: `no-line-break-after-else`
102→
103→## No line break before assignment 
104→
105→When a line is broken at an assignment (`=`) operator the break comes after the symbol.
106→
107→Rule id: `no-line-break-before-assignment`
108→
109→## No multi spaces
110→
111→Except in indentation and in KDoc's it is not allowed to have multiple consecutive spaces.
112→
113→Rule id: `no-multi-spaces`
114→
115→## No semicolons
116→
117→No semicolons (unless used to separate multiple statements on the same line).
118→
119→Rule id: `no-semi`
120→
121→## No trailing whitespaces
122→
123→Rule id: `no-trailing-spaces`
124→
125→## No `Unit` as return type 
126→
127→The `Unit` type is not allowed as return type of a function.
128→returns (`fun fn {}` instead of `fun fn: Unit {}`)
129→
130→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
131→
132→    ```kotlin
133→    fun fn() {}
134→    ```
135→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
136→
137→    ```kotlin
138→    fun fn(): Unit {}
139→    ```
140→
141→Rule id: `no-unit-return`
142→
143→## No unused imports
144→
145→Rule id: `no-unused-imports`
146→
147→## No wildcard imports
148→
149→No wildcard imports except imports listed in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`.
150→
151→!!! warning
152→
153→    In case property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` is not explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior. To disallow *all* wildcard imports, add property below to your `.editorconfig`:
154→    ```editorconfig
155→    [*.{kt,kts}]
156→    ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand = nothing
157→    ```
158→
159→Rule id: `no-wildcard-imports`
160→
161→## Package name
162→
163→Validates that the package name matches the regular expression `[a-z][a-zA-Z\d]*(\.[a-z][a-zA-Z\d]*)*`.
164→
165→Rule id: `package-name`
166→
167→## Parameter list wrapping
168→
169→When class/function signature doesn't fit on a single line, each parameter must be on a separate line
170→
171→Rule id: `parameter-list-wrapping`
172→
173→## Parameter wrapping
174→
175→When a function or class parameter doesn't fit on a single line, wrap the type or value to a separate line
176→
177→Rule id: `parameter-wrapping`
178→
179→## Property wrapping
180→
181→When a property doesn't fit on a single line, wrap the type or value to a separate line
182→
183→Rule id: `property-wrapping`
184→
185→## String template
186→
187→Consistent string templates (`$v` instead of `${v}`, `${p.v}` instead of `${p.v.toString()}`)
188→
189→Rule id: `string-template`
190→
191→## Trailing comma on call site
192→
193→Consistent removal (default) or adding of trailing comma's on call site.
194→
195→!!! important
196→    KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site` to configure the rule. When this property is enabled, KtLint *enforces* the usage of the trailing comma at call site while IntelliJ IDEA default formatter only *allows* to use the trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
197→
198→!!! note
199→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on call site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
200→

content.thinking:
I see the documentation explains the behavior. The documentation says:
- When `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` is not explicitly set, it allows wildcard imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior.

My change adds `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList()`, which means for the `ktlint_official` code style, no wildcard imports are allowed by default. This is the expected behavior according to the issue.

The documentation should probably be updated to mention that when using `ktlint_official` code style, no wildcard imports are allowed by default. But let me first verify my change works correctly by running a full test.

Let me also check if there are existing tests for code style-specific behavior in other rules that I can follow as a reference:

161→    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.  
162→
163→## Why is wildcard import `java.util.*` not reported by the `no-wildcard-imports` rule?
164→
165→The `no-wildcard-imports` rule forbids wildcard imports, except for imports defined in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`. If ths property is not set explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior.

content.thinking: Now let me also update the CHANGELOG.md to document this change. I should add an entry in the "Unreleased" section:

content.text: Let me also update the CHANGELOG.md to document this change:

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8→
  9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13→
 14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15→
 16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17→
 18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22→
 23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24→
 25→#### Experimental rules
 26→
 27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28→
 29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30→
 31→#### EditorConfig
 32→
 33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34→
 35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36→
 37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38→
 39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40→
 41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42→
 43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44→
 45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46→
 47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48→
 49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50→
 51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
