151→#### Custom Ruleset Provider `RuleSetProviderV2`
152→
153→Custom Rule Sets build for older versions of KtLint no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` interface has been replaced with `RuleSetProviderV3`. The accompanying interfaces `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` have been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` respectively.
154→
155→Contrary to `RuleSetProviderV2`, the `RuleSetProviderV3` no longer contains information about the rule set. About information now has to be specified in the new `Rule` class. This allows custom rule set providers to combine rules originating from different rule sets into a new rule set without loosing information about its origin. The typ of the id of the rule set is changed from `String` to `RuleSetId`. 
156→
157→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `RuleSetProviderV3` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.ruleset.core.api.RuleSetProviderV3`.
158→
159→The rule id's in `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` have been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". The rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Rules created by custom rule set providers and API Consumers should use a prefix other than `standard` to mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
160→
161→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
162→
163→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below: 
164→
165→```kotlin
166→public class SomeRule : Rule(
167→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
168→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
169→) {
170→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
171→
172→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
173→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
174→  }
175→  
176→  ...
177→}
178→```
179→
180→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
181→
182→#### Custom Reporter Provider `ReporterProvider`
183→
184→Custom Reporters build for older versions of KtLint are no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` interface has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`. The accompanying interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Reporter` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterV2`.
185→
186→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `ReporterProviderV2` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`.
187→
188→The biggest change in the `ReporterV2` is the replacement of the `LintError` class with `KtlintCliError` class. The latter class now contains a status field which more clearly explains the difference between a lint error which can be autocorrected versus a lint error that actually has been autocorrected.
189→
190→#### Custom rules provided by API Consumer
191→
192→API Consumers provide a set of rules directly to the Ktlint Rule Engine. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule`.
193→
194→The type of the rule id's has been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". Rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Custom rules created by the API Consumer should use a prefix other than `standard` to clearly mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
195→
196→Also, the field `About` has been added. This field specifies the name of the maintainer, and the repository url and issue tracker url of the rule.
197→
198→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
199→
200→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below:
201→
202→```kotlin
203→public class SomeRule : Rule(
204→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
205→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
206→) {
207→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
208→
209→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
210→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
211→  }
212→  
213→  ...
214→}
215→```
216→
217→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
218→
219→Like before, the API Consumer can still offer a mix of rules originating from `ktlint-ruleset-standard` as well as custom rules.
220→
221→#### `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` default value
222→
223→Previously the default value for `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` was set to `-1` in ktlint unless the property was defined explicitly in the `.editorconfig` or when `ktlint_code_style` was set to Android. As a result of that rules have to check that max_line_length contains a positive value before checking that the actual line length is exceeding the maximum. Now the value `Int.MAX_VALUE` (use constant `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF` to refer to that value) is used instead. 
224→
225→### Added
226→
227→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows a class to start with a blank line. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
228→* Add new experimental rule `if-else-bracing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rules enforces consistent usage of braces in all branches of a singe if or if-else-if statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
229→* Add new experimental rule `no-consecutive-comments` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
230→* Add new experimental rule `try-catch-finally-spacing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule enforces consistent spacing in try-catch, try-finally and try-catch-finally statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
231→* Wrap the type or value of a function or class parameter in case the maximum line length is exceeded `parameter-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
232→* Wrap the type or value of a property in case the maximum line length is exceeded `property-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
233→
234→### Removed
235→
236→* Remove support of the `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules`. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
237→
238→### Fixed
239→
240→* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
241→* Do not add the (first line of the) body expression on the same line as the function signature in case the max line length would be exceeded. `function-signature`. 
242→* Do not add the first line of a multiline body expression on the same line as the function signature in case function body expression wrapping property is set to `multiline`. `function-signature`. 
243→* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
244→* The parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list are aligned with first parameter whenever the first parameter is on the same line as the start of that function literal (not allowed in `ktlint_official` code style) `indent` ([#1756](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1756))
245→* Do not throw exception when enum class does not contain entries `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1711](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1711))
246→* Fix continuation indent for a dot qualified array access expression in `ktlint_official` code style only `indent` ([#1540](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1540))
247→* When generating the `.editorconfig` use value `off` for the `max_line_length` property instead of value `-1` to denote that lines are not restricted to a maximum length ([#1824](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1824))
248→* Do not report an "unnecessary semicolon" after adding a trailing comma to an enum class containing a code element after the last enum entry `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1786](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1786))
249→* A newline before a function return type should not be removed in case that leads to exceeding the maximum line length `function-return-type-spacing` ([#1764](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1764))
250→* Wrap annotations on type arguments in same way as with other constructs `annotation`, `wrapping` ([#1725](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1725))

267→## [0.48.2] - 2023-01-21
268→
269→### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
270→
271→Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
272→
273→API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
274→
275→Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
276→
277→The `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:
278→```editorconfig
279→[*.{kt,kts}]
280→ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *
281→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = true
282→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
283→```
284→Other projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
285→
286→Ktlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.
287→
288→From the Ktlint perspective, it is advised that API Consumers provide the default value. See example below, for how to specify the `editorConfigDefault` property:
289→```
290→KtLintRuleEngine(
291→    ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
292→    editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults(

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules

  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.importordering.PatternEntry
  9→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
 12→
 13→public class NoWildcardImportsRule :
 14→    StandardRule(
 15→        id = "no-wildcard-imports",
 16→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 17→            IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND,
 18→        ),
 19→    ) {
 20→    private lateinit var allowedWildcardImports: List<PatternEntry>
 21→
 22→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 23→        allowedWildcardImports = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND]
 24→    }
 25→
 26→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 27→        node: ASTNode,
 28→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 29→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 30→    ) {
 31→        if (node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
 32→            val importDirective = node.psi as KtImportDirective
 33→            val path = importDirective.importPath ?: return
 34→            if (!path.isAllUnder) return
 35→            if (allowedWildcardImports.none { it.matches(path) }) {
 36→                emit(node.startOffset, "Wildcard import", false)
 37→            }
 38→        }
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    public companion object {
 42→        private const val WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES = "*"
 43→        private const val WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES = "**"
 44→
 45→        private fun parseAllowedWildcardImports(allowedWildcardImports: String): List<PatternEntry> {
 46→            val importsList = allowedWildcardImports.split(",").onEach { it.trim() }
 47→
 48→            return importsList.map { import ->
 49→                if (import.endsWith(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES)) { // java.**
 50→                    PatternEntry(
 51→                        packageName = import.removeSuffix(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES).plus(WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES),
 52→                        withSubpackages = true,
 53→                        hasAlias = false,
 54→                    )
 55→                } else {
 56→                    PatternEntry(
 57→                        packageName = import,
 58→                        withSubpackages = false,
 59→                        hasAlias = false,
 60→                    )
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        private val PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
 66→            { _, value ->
 67→                when {
 68→                    else -> try {
 69→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
 70→                            value,
 71→                            value?.let(Companion::parseAllowedWildcardImports) ?: emptyList(),
 72→                        )
 73→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 74→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
 75→                            value,
 76→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
 77→                        )
 78→                    }
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→
 82→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
 83→            EditorConfigProperty(
 84→                type = PropertyType(
 85→                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
 86→                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
 87→                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
 88→                ),
 89→                /**
 90→                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
 91→                 * it's subpackages.
 92→                 *
 93→                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
 94→                 */
 95→                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
 96→                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList(),
 97→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
 98→            )
 99→    }
100→}
101→
102→public val NO_WILDCARD_IMPORTS_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoWildcardImportsRule().ruleId

160→!!! note
161→    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.  
162→
163→## Why is wildcard import `java.util.*` not reported by the `no-wildcard-imports` rule?
164→
165→The `no-wildcard-imports` rule forbids wildcard imports, except for imports defined in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`. If this property is not explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior. Note that this default does *not* apply to the `ktlint_official` code style for which *all* wildcard imports are forbidden when the property is not set.
166→

255→### Changed
256→* In the `ktlint_official` code style, the default value for the `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` is changed to `unset`. As a result, wildcard imports like `java.util.*` are no longer allowed by default when using the `ktlint_official` code style `no-wildcard-imports` ([#1792](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1792))

258→  Changelog:
259→* KtLint CLI exits with an error in any of following cases (this list is not exhaustive):
260→  - A custom ruleset jar is to be loaded and that jar contains a deprecated RuleSetProviderV2.
261→  - A custom ruleset jar is to be loaded and that jar does not contain the required RuleSetProviderV3.
262→  - A custom reporter jar is to be loaded and that jar contains a deprecated ReporterProvider.
263→  - A custom reporter jar is to be loaded and that jar does not contain the required ReporterProviderV2.
264→* Disable the default patterns if the option `--patterns-from-stdin` is specified ([#1793](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1793))
265→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.20-Beta` and Kotlin version to `1.8.10`.
266→* Revert to matrix build to speed up build, especially for the Windows related build ([#1787](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1787))

content.thinking:
All my changes look good. Let me summarize what I've done:

1. **Modified `NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`**: Added `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList()` to the `IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND` EditorConfigProperty. This means that when using the `ktlint_official` code style, no wildcard imports are allowed by default (the list of allowed wildcard imports is empty), while for other code styles (like `intellij_idea` and `android_studio`), the default remains `java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**`.

2. **Updated `docs/rules/standard.md`**: Added a note that the default wildcard import exception does not apply to the `ktlint_official` code style.

3. **Updated `docs/faq.md`**: Added a note explaining that the default wildcard import exception does not apply to the `ktlint_official` code style.

4. **Updated `CHANGELOG.md`**: Added an entry in the "Changed" section documenting this behavior change.

 27→class CommandLineTestRunner(private val tempDir: Path) {
 28→    private val ktlintCli: String = System.getProperty("ktlint-cli")
 29→
 30→    /**
 31→     * Run ktlint CLI in a separate process. All files in directory [testProjectName] are copied to a temporary
 32→     * directory. Paths in the [arguments] that refer to files in directory [testProjectName] need to be prefixed with
 33→     * the placeholder [BASE_DIR_PLACEHOLDER] to obtain a fully qualified path. During the test execution this
 34→     * placeholder is replaces with the actual directory name that is created for that unit test.
 35→     */
 36→    fun run(
 37→        testProjectName: String,
 38→        arguments: List<String> = emptyList(),
 39→        stdin: InputStream? = null,
 40→        executionAssertions: ExecutionResult.() -> Unit,
 41→    ) {
 42→        val projectPath = prepareTestProject(testProjectName)
 43→        // Forking in a new shell process, so 'ktlint' will pick up new 'PATH' env variable value
 44→        val process =
 45→            ProcessBuilder(*interpreterPathAndArgs(), ktlintCommand(arguments))
 46→                .apply {
 47→                    prependPathWithJavaBinHome()
 48→                    directory(projectPath.toAbsolutePath().toFile())
 49→                }.start()
 50→
 51→        if (stdin != null) {
 52→            process.outputStream.use(stdin::copyTo)
 53→        }
 54→
 55→        // Give the process some time to complete
 56→        (0..WAIT_INTERVAL_MAX_OCCURRENCES).forEach { _ ->
 57→            if (process.isAlive) {
 58→                // Check regularly whether the ktlint command has finished to speed up the unit testing
 59→                process.waitFor(WAIT_INTERVAL_DURATION, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)
 60→            }
 61→        }
 62→
 63→        // Get the output from the process before destroying it as otherwise the streams are not collected completely
 64→        val output = process.inputStream.bufferedReader().use { it.readLines() }
 65→        val error = process.errorStream.bufferedReader().use { it.readLines() }
 66→
 67→        if (process.isAlive) {
 68→            // Destroy the process as it is still running
 69→            process.destroyForcibly()
 70→
 71→            // For unknown reasons the process sometimes results in a timeout although the ktlint command is terminated when the last line
 72→            // of the output contains the debug line containing the exit code. If this line is found, consider it as a normal termination.
 73→            val exitCode =
 74→                output
 75→                    .lastOrNull { it.contains(EXIT_KTLINT_WITH_EXIT_CODE_DEBUG_LINE) }
 76→                    ?.substringAfter(EXIT_KTLINT_WITH_EXIT_CODE_DEBUG_LINE)
 77→                    ?.toInt()
 78→            if (exitCode != null) {
 79→                executionAssertions(ExecutionResult(exitCode, output, error, projectPath))
 80→            } else {

 82→                val maxDurationInSeconds = (WAIT_INTERVAL_DURATION * WAIT_INTERVAL_MAX_OCCURRENCES).div(1000.0)

 89→                            ),

 97→            process.destroy()

132→    private fun ktlintCommand(arguments: List<String>): String =
133→        mutableListOf<String>()
134→            .apply {
135→                if (isWindows()) {
136→                    // KtLint is not an executable command on Windows OS
137→                    add("java")
138→
139→                    val javaVersion = System.getProperty("java.specification.version").javaVersionAsInt()
140→                    if (javaVersion != null) {

142→                        if (javaVersion >= 16) {

155→                add(ktlintCli)

157→                // Always run with debug logging as this is convenient when test fails and when ktlint is finished it
158→                // prints the log line "Finished in ###ms / ... " which is used as fallback to determine whether ktlint
159→                // did finish correctly.
160→                add("-l=trace")

162→                addAll(arguments)
163→            }.joinToString(separator = " ")

167→        if (this == null) {

170→
171→        val matchResult = JAVA_VERSION_REGEX.matchEntire(this)

175→            ?: return toIntOrNull()

217→                    val destinationFile = dest.resolve(relativeFile)

219→                    file.copyTo(destinationFile)

content.thinking:
Looking at the test, it copies the test project to a temporary directory and runs ktlint on it. The test checks that the exit code is 0 and the error output is empty. The problem is that when running ktlint, it might be picking up the `.editorconfig` from the project root which sets `ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official`.

Wait, looking at line 107, it copies the test project to a temp directory and runs ktlint there. The temp directory should not have the project's `.editorconfig`, so this should be isolated.

Let me look more carefully at the test failure. The test is asserting that `errorOutput.isEmpty()`. Let me check if there's any .editorconfig file that might be affecting the tests.

Actually, looking at line 47-49 of CommandLineTestRunner.kt:
```kotlin
.apply {
    prependPathWithJavaBinHome()
    directory(projectPath.toAbsolutePath().toFile())
}.start()
```

It sets the directory to the project path which is a temp directory. Without any `.editorconfig` in that temp directory, ktlint would use the default code style (`intellij_idea`), not `ktlint_official`.

The test failure might be unrelated to my changes. Let me re-run the test by itself to see if it's flaky:

content.thinking:
All the NoWildcardImportsRule tests pass, which confirms that my implementation is correct. The CLI test failure is an environmental issue unrelated to my changes.Let me summarize the changes I've made:

1. **Code change in `NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`**: Added `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList()` to the `IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND` EditorConfigProperty. This ensures that when using the `ktlint_official` code style, no wildcard imports are allowed by default (empty list), while other code styles (`intellij_idea`, `android_studio`) retain the existing defaults (`java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**`).

2. **Documentation update in `docs/rules/standard.md`**: Added a clarification that the default wildcard import exception does not apply to the `ktlint_official` code style.

3. **Documentation update in `docs/faq.md`**: Added a note explaining that the default wildcard import exception does not apply to the `ktlint_official` code style, for which all wildcard imports are forbidden when the property is not set.

4. **CHANGELOG update in `CHANGELOG.md`**: Added an entry documenting this behavior change in the "Changed" section.

The implementation follows the existing pattern used by other EditorConfigProperty definitions that have different defaults for different code styles. The fix is minimal and targeted, only affecting the `ktlint_official` code style while preserving backward compatibility for other code styles.

content.text:
All the NoWildcardImportsRule tests pass. The CLI test failure is an environmental issue (the `_JAVA_OPTIONS` message being printed to stderr) that is completely unrelated to my changes.

Let me provide a summary of the changes I've made to resolve the issue:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. Code Change: `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`

Added `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList()` to the `IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND` EditorConfigProperty definition. This ensures that:

- **For `ktlint_official` code style**: No wildcard imports are allowed by default (empty list)
- **For `intellij_idea` and `android_studio` code styles**: The existing defaults (`java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**`) are preserved for backward compatibility

### 2. Documentation Update: `docs/rules/standard.md`

Updated the "No wildcard imports" section to clarify that the default wildcard import exception does not apply to the `ktlint_official` code style.

### 3. Documentation Update: `docs/faq.md`

Updated the FAQ entry "Why is wildcard import `java.util.*` not reported by the `no-wildcard-imports` rule?" to note that the `ktlint_official` code style forbids all wildcard imports when the property is not set.

### 4. CHANGELOG Update: `CHANGELOG.md`

Added an entry in the "Changed" section documenting this behavior change with a reference to the GitHub issue #1792.

---

The fix is minimal and targeted, following the existing pattern used by other `EditorConfigProperty` definitions in the codebase that have different defaults for different code styles. This change only affects the `ktlint_official` code style while preserving full backward compatibility for other code styles.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.importordering.PatternEntry
  9→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
 12→
 13→public class NoWildcardImportsRule :
 14→    StandardRule(
 15→        id = "no-wildcard-imports",
 16→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 17→            IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND,
 18→        ),
 19→    ) {
 20→    private lateinit var allowedWildcardImports: List<PatternEntry>
 21→
 22→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 23→        allowedWildcardImports = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND]
 24→    }
 25→
 26→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 27→        node: ASTNode,
 28→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 29→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 30→    ) {
 31→        if (node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
 32→            val importDirective = node.psi as KtImportDirective
 33→            val path = importDirective.importPath ?: return
 34→            if (!path.isAllUnder) return
 35→            if (allowedWildcardImports.none { it.matches(path) }) {
 36→                emit(node.startOffset, "Wildcard import", false)
 37→            }
 38→        }
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    public companion object {
 42→        private const val WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES = "*"
 43→        private const val WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES = "**"
 44→
 45→        private fun parseAllowedWildcardImports(allowedWildcardImports: String): List<PatternEntry> {
 46→            val importsList = allowedWildcardImports.split(",").onEach { it.trim() }
 47→
 48→            return importsList.map { import ->
 49→                if (import.endsWith(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES)) { // java.**
 50→                    PatternEntry(
 51→                        packageName = import.removeSuffix(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES).plus(WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES),
 52→                        withSubpackages = true,
 53→                        hasAlias = false,
 54→                    )
 55→                } else {
 56→                    PatternEntry(
 57→                        packageName = import,
 58→                        withSubpackages = false,
 59→                        hasAlias = false,
 60→                    )
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        private val PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
 66→            { _, value ->
 67→                when {
 68→                    else -> try {
 69→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
 70→                            value,
 71→                            value?.let(Companion::parseAllowedWildcardImports) ?: emptyList(),
 72→                        )
 73→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 74→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
 75→                            value,
 76→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
 77→                        )
 78→                    }
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→
 82→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
 83→            EditorConfigProperty(
 84→                type = PropertyType(
 85→                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
 86→                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
 87→                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
 88→                ),
 89→                /**
 90→                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
 91→                 * it's subpackages.
 92→                 *
 93→                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
 94→                 */
 95→                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
 96→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
 97→            )
 98→    }
 99→}
100→
101→public val NO_WILDCARD_IMPORTS_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoWildcardImportsRule().ruleId
102→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2→
 3→import org.ec4j.core.model.Property
 4→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * Definition of '.editorconfig' property enriched with fields required by the [com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine].
 8→ *
 9→ * Properties can be:
10→ * - universal `.editorconfig` properties defined
11→ *   [here](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/wiki/EditorConfig-Properties#current-universal-properties)
12→ * - universal IntelliJ IDEA properties defined
13→ *   [here](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-community/blob/master/platform/lang-api/src/com/intellij/psi/codeStyle/CommonCodeStyleSettings.java)
14→ * - Kotlin specific properties defined
15→ *   [here](https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/master/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java)
16→ */
17→public data class EditorConfigProperty<T>(
18→    /**
19→     * Type of property. Could be one of default ones (see [PropertyType.STANDARD_TYPES]) or custom one.
20→     */
21→    public val type: PropertyType<T>,
22→
23→    /**
24→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties. This default applies to all code styles
25→     * unless the code style specific default value is set.
26→     */
27→    public val defaultValue: T,
28→
29→    /**
30→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'ktlint_official'. When not
31→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
32→     */
33→    public val ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
34→
35→    /**
36→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'intellij_idea'. When not
37→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
38→     */
39→    public val intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
40→
41→    /**
42→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'android_studio'. When not
43→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
44→     */
45→    public val androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
46→
47→    /**
48→     * If set, it maps the actual value set for the property, to another valid value for that property. See example
49→     * below where
50→     * ```kotlin
51→     * propertyMapper = { property, isAndroidCodeStyle ->
52→     *     when {
53→     *         property == null ->
54→     *             // property is not defined in ".editorconfig" file
55→     *         property.isUnset ->
56→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with special value "unset"
57→     *         property.sourceValue == "some-string-value" ->
58→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with a value that needs to be remapped to another
59→     *             // valid value. For example the "max_line_length" property accepts value "off" but is remapped to
60→     *             // "-1" in ktlint.
61→     *        else ->
62→     *             property.getValueAs() // or null
63→     *     }
64→     * }
65→     * ```
66→     * In case the lambda returns a null value then, the code style specific default value or the generic [defaultValue]
67→     * is used.
68→     */
69→    public val propertyMapper: ((Property?, CodeStyleValue) -> T?)? = null,
70→
71→    /**
72→     * Custom function that represents [T] as String. Defaults to the standard `toString()` call. Override the
73→     * default implementation in case you need a different behavior than the standard `toString()` (e.g. for
74→     * collections joinToString() is more applicable).
75→     */
76→    public val propertyWriter: (T) -> String = { it.toString() },
77→
78→    /**
79→     * Optional message to be displayed whenever the value of the property is being retrieved while it has been deprecated.
80→     */
81→    val deprecationWarning: String? = null,
82→
83→    /**
84→     * Optional message to be displayed whenever the value of the property is being retrieved while it has been deprecated.
85→     */
86→    val deprecationError: String? = null,
87→
88→    /**
89→     * Name of the property. A property must be named in case multiple properties are defined for the same type.
90→     * Defaults to the name of the type when not set.
91→     */
92→    public val name: String = type.name,
93→)

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  5→import mu.KotlinLogging
  6→import org.ec4j.core.model.Property
  7→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
  8→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValue
  9→
 10→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * Loaded [Property]s from `.editorconfig` files.
 14→ */
 15→public data class EditorConfig(
 16→    private val properties: Map<String, Property> = emptyMap(),
 17→) {
 18→    public constructor(vararg properties: Property) : this(
 19→        properties.associateBy { property -> property.name },
 20→    )
 21→
 22→    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue
 23→        get() =
 24→            with(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY) {
 25→                // Prevent printing a lot of warnings when the code_style_value is not defined explicitly by not using the function to get
 26→                // the default value for the property
 27→                type
 28→                    .getPropertyValue(type.name)
 29→                    .parsed
 30→                    ?: defaultValue
 31→            }
 32→
 33→    /**
 34→     * Gets the value of [editorConfigProperty] from [EditorConfig] provided that the name of the property is identical to the name of the
 35→     * type of the property. If the value is not found, the default value for the matching code style is returned.
 36→     */
 37→    public operator fun <T> get(editorConfigProperty: EditorConfigProperty<T>): T {
 38→        when {
 39→            editorConfigProperty.deprecationError != null ->
 40→                throw DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(
 41→                    "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is disallowed: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationError}",
 42→                )
 43→
 44→            editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning != null ->
 45→                LOGGER.warn { "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is deprecated: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning}" }
 46→        }
 47→        val property = properties.getOrElse(editorConfigProperty.name) {
 48→            throw IllegalStateException(
 49→                "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' can not be retrieved from this EditorConfig. Note that the EditorConfig which " +
 50→                    "is provided to class '${Rule::class.qualifiedName}' only contains the properties which are defined in the property " +
 51→                    "'${Rule::usesEditorConfigProperties.name}'.",
 52→            )
 53→        }
 54→
 55→        editorConfigProperty
 56→            .propertyMapper
 57→            ?.invoke(property, codeStyle)
 58→            ?.let { newValue ->
 59→                // If the property value is remapped to a non-null value then return it immediately.
 60→                val originalValue = property.sourceValue
 61→                if (newValue.toString() != originalValue) {
 62→                    LOGGER.trace {
 63→                        "Value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is remapped " +
 64→                            "from '$originalValue' to '$newValue'"
 65→                    }
 66→                }
 67→                return newValue
 68→            }
 69→
 70→        return if (property.isUnset) {
 71→            editorConfigProperty
 72→                .getDefaultValue()
 73→                .also {
 74→                    LOGGER.trace {
 75→                        "No value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' was found. Value has been defaulted to " +
 76→                            "'$it'. Setting the value explicitly in '.editorconfig' removes this message from the log."
 77→                    }
 78→                }
 79→        } else {
 80→            // In case the EditorConfig is loaded incorrectly it can happen that the type field of the loaded property is null. Getting the
 81→            // value via "property.getValueAs()" can lead to a class cast exception as the actual type of the value returned is transformed
 82→            // to a String instead of type T. Getting the value via the editorConfigProperty type is safe as it can not be null.
 83→            editorConfigProperty.getParsedValueOrDefault() ?: editorConfigProperty.getDefaultValue()
 84→        }
 85→    }
 86→
 87→    /**
 88→     * Gets the value of the property with [propertyType] and name [propertyName] from [EditorConfig]. Returns null if the type is not
 89→     * found. Also, returns null when no property with the name is found.
 90→     */
 91→    public fun <T> getEditorConfigValueOrNull(
 92→        propertyType: PropertyType<T>,
 93→        propertyName: String,
 94→    ): T? = propertyType.getPropertyValue(propertyName).parsed
 95→
 96→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getDefaultValue() =
 97→        when (codeStyle) {
 98→            CodeStyleValue.android -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
 99→            CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
100→            CodeStyleValue.official -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
101→            CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
102→            CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue
103→        }
104→
105→    /**
106→     * Checks whether a property with name [propertyName] is defined.
107→     */
108→    public fun contains(propertyName: String): Boolean = properties.contains(propertyName)
109→
110→    /**
111→     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType]. Returns null if the property type is not found. Can only be used for
112→     * properties for which the name of the property is identical to the name of the type.
113→     */
114→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(): T? =
115→        this.type.getParsedValueOrDefault(this.type.name, this.defaultValue)
116→
117→    /**
118→     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType] and name [propertyName]. Returns null if the property type is not
119→     * found. Intended to be used for properties for which the name is not identical to the name of the property type.
120→     */
121→    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(
122→        propertyName: String,
123→        defaultValue: T,
124→    ): T =
125→        getPropertyValue(propertyName)
126→            .let { propertyValue ->
127→                if (propertyValue.isValid) {
128→                    propertyValue.parsed
129→                } else {
130→                    LOGGER.warn {
131→                        "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' contains an invalid value '${propertyValue.source}'. The default value " +
132→                            "'$defaultValue' is used instead."
133→                    }
134→                    defaultValue
135→                }
136→            }
137→
138→    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getPropertyValue(propertyName: String): PropertyValue<T> =
139→        parse(
140→            properties[propertyName]?.sourceValue,
141→        )
142→
143→    /**
144→     * Maps all properties with given [mapper] to a collection.
145→     */
146→    public fun <T> map(mapper: (Property) -> T): Collection<T> =
147→        properties
148→            .values
149→            .map { mapper(it) }
150→
151→    /**
152→     * Adds the given [EditorConfigProperty]'s with its default value for the active code style to the [EditorConfig] if the property does
153→     * not yet exist.
154→     */
155→    public fun addPropertiesWithDefaultValueIfMissing(vararg defaultEditorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperty<*>): EditorConfig {
156→        val editorConfigProperties = defaultEditorConfigProperties.toSet()
157→        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
158→        return editorConfigProperties
159→            .defaultProperties()
160→            .plus(
161→                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
162→                properties,
163→            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
164→    }
165→
166→    /**
167→     * Creates an [EditorConfig] containing [editorConfigProperties] only. Retains the value of the property when defined in the original
168→     * [EditorConfig]. If the property does not exist in the original [EditorConfig], it will be added and its value is set to the default
169→     * value of the active code style.
170→     */
171→    public fun filterBy(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>): EditorConfig {
172→        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
173→        return editorConfigProperties
174→            .defaultProperties()
175→            .plus(
176→                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
177→                properties.filterBy(editorConfigProperties.map { it.name }),
178→            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
179→    }
180→
181→    private fun requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>) {
182→        val editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities =
183→            editorConfigProperties
184→                .groupBy { it.name }
185→                .filterValues { groupedEditorConfigProperties ->
186→                    groupedEditorConfigProperties
187→                        .distinctBy { it.hashCode() }
188→                        .count() > 1
189→                }
190→        require(editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities.isEmpty()) {
191→            editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities
192→                .map { (name, editorConfigProperties) ->
193→                    editorConfigProperties
194→                        .joinToString(
195→                            separator = "\n",
196→                            prefix = "Found multiple editorconfig properties with name '$name' but having distinct identities:\n",
197→                        ) { "  - $it" }
198→                }.joinToString(separator = "\n")
199→        }
200→    }
201→
202→    private fun Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>.defaultProperties(): Map<String, Property> {
203→        return associate { editorConfigProperty ->
204→            editorConfigProperty
205→                .writeDefaultValue()
206→                .let { editorConfigProperty.name to editorConfigProperty.toPropertyWithValue(it) }
207→        }
208→    }
209→
210→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.writeDefaultValue() = propertyWriter(getDefaultValue())
211→
212→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.toPropertyWithValue(value: String): Property =
213→        Property
214→            .builder()
215→            .type(type)
216→            .name(name)
217→            .value(value)
218→            .build()
219→
220→    private fun Map<String, Property>.filterBy(editorConfigProperties: List<String>): Map<String, Property> =
221→        filterKeys { it in editorConfigProperties }
222→}
223→
224→private class DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(message: String) : RuntimeException(message)
225→

  9→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
 12→
 13→public class NoWildcardImportsRule :
 14→    StandardRule(
 15→        id = "no-wildcard-imports",
 16→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 17→            IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND,
 18→        ),
 19→    ) {
 20→    private lateinit var allowedWildcardImports: List<PatternEntry>
 21→
 22→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 23→        allowedWildcardImports = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND]
 24→    }
 25→
 26→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 27→        node: ASTNode,
 28→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 29→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 30→    ) {
 31→        if (node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
 32→            val importDirective = node.psi as KtImportDirective
 33→            val path = importDirective.importPath ?: return
 34→            if (!path.isAllUnder) return
 35→            if (allowedWildcardImports.none { it.matches(path) }) {
 36→                emit(node.startOffset, "Wildcard import", false)
 37→            }
 38→        }
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    public companion object {
 42→        private const val WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES = "*"
 43→        private const val WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES = "**"
 44→
 45→        private fun parseAllowedWildcardImports(allowedWildcardImports: String): List<PatternEntry> {
 46→            val importsList = allowedWildcardImports.split(",").onEach { it.trim() }
 47→
 48→            return importsList.map { import ->
 49→                if (import.endsWith(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES)) { // java.**
 50→                    PatternEntry(
 51→                        packageName = import.removeSuffix(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES).plus(WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES),
 52→                        withSubpackages = true,
 53→                        hasAlias = false,
 54→                    )
 55→                } else {
 56→                    PatternEntry(
 57→                        packageName = import,
 58→                        withSubpackages = false,
 59→                        hasAlias = false,
 60→                    )
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        private val PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
 66→            { _, value ->
 67→                when {
 68→                    else -> try {
 69→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
 70→                            value,
 71→                            value?.let(Companion::parseAllowedWildcardImports) ?: emptyList(),
 72→                        )
 73→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 74→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
 75→                            value,
 76→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
 77→                        )
 78→                    }
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→
 82→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
 83→            EditorConfigProperty(
 84→                type = PropertyType(
 85→                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
 86→                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
 87→                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
 88→                ),
 89→                /**
 90→                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
 91→                 * it's subpackages.
 92→                 *
 93→                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
 94→                 */
 95→                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
 96→                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList(),
 97→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
 98→            )
 99→    }
100→}

110→    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): KtLintAssertThat {

284→    private fun ktLintAssertThatAssertable(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
285→        if (editorConfigProperties.isEmpty()) {

287→                ruleProvider = ruleProvider,

301→    private fun code() =
302→        if (filePath != null) {

304→            // the Ktlint test file system.
305→            val path = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.resolve(filePath)
306→            KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.writeFile(path.pathString, code)

312→    public companion object {
313→        /**
314→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
315→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules.
316→         */
317→        public fun assertThatRule(provider: () -> Rule): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat()
318→
319→        /**
320→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
321→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules. The rules provided via [additionalRuleProviders] are only
322→         * executed during the format phase of the test. This means that the unit test only has to check the lint
323→         * violations thrown by the rule for which the assertThat is created. But the code is formatted by both the rule
324→         * and the rules provided by [additionalRuleProviders] in the order as defined by the rule definitions.
325→         */
326→
327→        public fun assertThatRule(
328→            provider: () -> Rule,
329→            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
330→        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat(additionalRuleProviders)
331→
332→        private fun RuleProvider.assertThat(additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet()): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat =
333→            { code ->
334→                KtLintAssertThat(
335→                    ruleProvider = this,
336→                    code = code,
337→                    additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toMutableSet(),
338→                )
339→            }
340→
341→        /**
342→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
343→         */
344→        public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER: String = "Max line length marker:"
345→
346→        /**
347→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
348→         */
349→        public const val EOL_CHAR: Char = '#'
350→
351→        init {
352→            /* A unit test using the max line length marker typically looks like:
353→             * val code =
354→             *     """
355→             *     // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
356→             *     val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
357→             *     """.trimIndent()
358→             * In order for this to work properly, the length of "$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER" must be identical to the length of the real value
359→             * of the constant MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.
360→             */
361→            require("$".plus(::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name).length == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.length) {
362→                "Length of the value of the string '${'$'}MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' must be identical to length of the value of the " +
363→                    "constant. Tests using this constant will not work as intended otherwise."
364→            }
365→        }
366→    }

369→/**
370→ * Immutable assertable. Once the first assertion is made on [KtLintAssertThat] it is converted to the
371→ * [KtLintAssertThatAssertable] which allows no further modifications of the internal state. This guarantees that all
372→ * assertions operate on the same state.
373→ */
374→public class KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
375→    /** The provider of the rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created. */
376→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
377→    private val code: Code,
378→    private val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
379→    /**
380→     *  The rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note that
381→     *  lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
382→     */
383→    private val additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
384→) : AbstractAssert<KtLintAssertThatAssertable, String>(code.content, KtLintAssertThatAssertable::class.java) {
385→    private val ruleId = ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance().ruleId
386→
387→    /**
388→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s caused by the rule associated with the
389→     * KtLintAssertThat.
390→     *
391→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
392→     */
393→    public fun hasNoLintViolations() {
394→        assertThat(lint().filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
395→
396→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
397→        val (actualFormattedCode, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
398→
399→        assertAll(
400→            {
401→                assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting)
402→                    .describedAs(
403→                        "LintViolations found by format while no lint errors were found during Lint. " +
404→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
405→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
406→                            } else {
407→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
408→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
409→                            },
410→                    )
411→                    .isEmpty()
412→            },
413→            {
414→                assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
415→                    .describedAs(
416→                        "Code is changed by format while no lint errors were found. " +
417→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
418→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
419→                            } else {
420→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
421→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
422→                            },
423→                    )
424→                    .isEqualTo(code.content)
425→            },
426→        )
427→    }
428→
429→    /**
430→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for the given rule id.
431→     */
432→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
433→        val (_, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
434→
435→        assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting.filter { it.ruleId == ruleId })
436→            .describedAs("At least 1 lint violation was found for rule id '$ruleId' while none were expected")
437→            .isEmpty()
438→
439→        return this
440→    }
441→
442→    /**
443→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s except in the additional formatting rules.
444→     *
445→     * Note that this method can and should be chained with [isFormattedAs] to verify whether the code is correctly
446→     * formatted.
447→     *
448→     * This method can be used when the rule which is associated with the KtLintAssertThat is not violated by the sample
449→     * code, but the code is reformatted by the additional formatting rules. In case that rules are dependent on each
450→     * other, a unit test cal still verify that code is formatted correctly even when the rule under test is not
451→     * violated.
452→     */
453→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
454→        check(additionalRuleProviders.isNotEmpty()) {
455→            "hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules can only be used when additional rules have been added"
456→        }
457→
458→        val lintErrors = lint()
459→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
460→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterAdditionalRulesOnly()).isNotEmpty
461→
462→        return this
463→    }
464→
465→    /**
466→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]. This is a sugar coated version of
467→     * [hasLintViolation] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
468→     */
469→    public fun hasLintViolation(
470→        line: Int,
471→        col: Int,
472→        detail: String,
473→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
474→        hasLintViolations(
475→            LintViolation(
476→                line = line,
477→                col = col,
478→                detail = detail,
479→            ),
480→        )
481→
482→    /**
483→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] caused by an additional rule. This is a sugar coated
484→     * version of [hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
485→     */
486→    public fun hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(
487→        line: Int,
488→        col: Int,
489→        detail: String,
490→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
491→        hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(
492→            LintViolation(
493→                line = line,
494→                col = col,
495→                detail = detail,
496→            ),
497→        )
498→
499→    /**
500→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s which can be automatically corrected.
501→     */
502→    public fun hasLintViolations(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
503→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
504→
505→        val actualLintViolationFields =
506→            lint()
507→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
508→                .toLintViolationsFields()
509→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
510→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
511→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields(canBeAutoCorrected = true))
512→        return this
513→    }
514→
515→    /**
516→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s caused by additional rules and which can be
517→     * automatically corrected.
518→     */
519→    public fun hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
520→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
521→
522→        val actualLintViolationFields =
523→            lint()
524→                .filterAdditionalRulesOnly()
525→                .toLintViolationsFields()
526→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
527→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
528→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields(canBeAutoCorrected = true))
529→        return this
530→    }
531→
532→    /**
533→     * Asserts that the code is formatted as given.
534→     */
535→    public fun isFormattedAs(formattedCode: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
536→        check(formattedCode != code.content) {
537→            "Use '.hasNoLintViolations()' instead of '.isFormattedAs(<original code>)'"
538→        }
539→
540→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
541→        val (actualFormattedCode, _) = format()
542→
543→        assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
544→            .describedAs("Code is formatted as")
545→            .isEqualTo(formattedCode)
546→
547→        return this
548→    }
549→
550→    /**
551→     * Asserts that the code contains the given [LintViolation] which can not be automatically corrected.
552→     */
553→    public fun hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(
554→        line: Int,
555→        col: Int,
556→        detail: String,
557→    ): Unit =
558→        hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(
559→            LintViolation(
560→                line = line,
561→                col = col,
562→                detail = detail,
563→            ),
564→        )
565→
566→    /**
567→     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation]s and that those violations can not be automatically
568→     * corrected.
569→     */
570→    public fun hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(vararg expectedLintViolations: LintViolation) {
571→        check(expectedLintViolations.isNotEmpty())
572→
573→        val actualLintViolationFields =
574→            lint()
575→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
576→                .toLintViolationsFields()
577→
578→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
579→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can not be automatically corrected")
580→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedLintViolations.toLintViolationsFields(canBeAutoCorrected = false))
581→    }

583→    private fun Array<out LintViolation>.toLintViolationsFields(canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean): Array<LintViolationFields> {
584→        return map {
585→            LintViolationFields(
586→                line = it.line,
587→                col = it.col,
588→                detail = it.detail,
589→                canBeAutoCorrected = canBeAutoCorrected,
590→            )
591→        }.toTypedArray()
592→    }

594→    private fun List<LintError>.filterAdditionalRulesOnly() = filter { it.ruleId != ruleId.value }
595→
596→    private fun List<LintError>.filterCurrentRuleOnly() = filter { it.ruleId == ruleId.value }
597→
598→    private fun List<LintError>.toLintViolationsFields(): Array<LintViolationFields> =
599→        map {
600→            LintViolationFields(
601→                line = it.line,
602→                col = it.col,
603→                detail = it.detail,
604→                canBeAutoCorrected = it.canBeAutoCorrected,
605→            )
606→        }.toTypedArray()

608→    private fun createKtLintRuleEngine(): KtLintRuleEngine {
609→        val ruleProviders =
610→            setOf(ruleProvider)
611→                // Also run the additional rules as the main rule might have a VisitorModifier which requires one or more of
612→                // the additional rules to be loaded and enabled as well.
613→                .plus(additionalRuleProviders)
614→                .plus(DUMP_AST_RULE_PROVIDER)
615→        val editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride
616→            .enableExperimentalRules()
617→            .extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders)
618→        return KtLintRuleEngine(
619→            ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
620→            editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride,
621→            fileSystem = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.fileSystem,
622→        )
623→    }
624→
625→    private fun lint(): List<LintError> {
626→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
627→        createKtLintRuleEngine().lint(code) { lintError -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
628→        return lintErrors
629→    }
630→
631→    private fun format(): Pair<String, List<LintError>> {
632→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
633→        val formattedCode = createKtLintRuleEngine().format(code) { lintError, _ -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
634→        return Pair(formattedCode, lintErrors)
635→    }
636→
637→    /* Representation of the field of the [LintError] that should be identical. Note that no comparison can be made
638→     * against the original [LintError] as the [canBeAutoCorrected] flag is excluded from the hashcode.
639→     */
640→    private data class LintViolationFields(
641→        val line: Int,
642→        val col: Int,
643→        val detail: String,
644→        val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean,
645→    )
646→}

652→    '${::EOL_CHAR.name}' which indicates the last position that is allowed.

658→ * derived from the rule for which the AssertThat was created.

660→public data class LintViolation(

666→/**
667→ * Enables the rule sets for the given set of [ruleProviders] unless the rule execution of that rule set was already
668→ * provided.
669→ */
670→private fun EditorConfigOverride.extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigOverride {
671→    val ruleSetRuleExecutions = ruleProviders
672→        .asSequence()
673→        .map { ruleProvider ->
674→            ruleProvider
675→                .createNewRuleInstance()
676→                .ruleId
677→                .ruleSetId
678→                .createRuleSetExecutionEditorConfigProperty()
679→        }.distinct()
680→        .filter { editorConfigProperty -> this.properties[editorConfigProperty] == null }
681→        .map { it to RuleExecution.enabled }
682→        .toList()
683→        .toTypedArray()
684→    return this.plus(*ruleSetRuleExecutions)
685→}
686→
687→private fun EditorConfigOverride.enableExperimentalRules(): EditorConfigOverride =
688→    plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)
